name: parseidf

metadata.name: parseidf
metadata.summary: A module for parsing EnergyPlus IDF files

documentation:
# parseidf.py

Parses an IDF file (as used by EnergyPlus) into a dictionary of lists in
the following manner:

- each IDF object is represented by a list of its fields, with the
  first field being the object type.
- each such list is appended to a list of objects with the same type in
  the dictionary, indexed by type:

{ A => \[[A, x, y, z], [A, a, b, c]\], B => \[[B, 1, 2], [B, 1, 2, 3]\] }

- the index keys are all capitalized.
- also, all field values are strings, i.e. no interpretation of the
  values is made.

# Example

```
import parseidf

with open(r'in.idf', 'r') as f:
    idf = parseidf.parse(f.read())
print idf.keys()  # lists the object types in the idf file
print idf['OUTPUT:VARIABLE']  # lists all the Output:Variable objects in the idf file
```

# Dependencies

parseidf depends on PLY (Python Lex & Yacc):
<https://pypi.python.org/pypi/ply/3.4>

modules.name: parseidf
documentation:
parseidf.py


parses an idf file into a dictionary of lists in the following manner:

    each idf object is represented by a list of its fields, with the first
    field being the objects type.

    each such list is appended to a list of objects with the same type in the
    dictionary, indexed by type:

    { [A] => [ [A, x, y, z], [A, a, b, c],
      [B] => [ [B, 1, 2], [B, 1, 2, 3] }

    also, all field values are strings, i.e. no interpretation of the values is
    made.

functions.name: parseidf.t_COMMENT
documentation: ignore comments, tracking line numbers at the same time

functions.name: parseidf.t_newline
documentation: Define a rule so we can track line numbers

functions.name: parseidf.parse
documentation:
parses a string with the contents of the idf file and returns the
dictionary representation.
